message:
chore(beads): record bd-31lp8 closure for extension write confinement fix

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -663,6 +663,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-31hg","title":"Replace manual history navigation with bubbles::List component","description":"Use bubbles::List instead of manual implementation for history navigation in TUI.

## Current State
- Interactive TUI has manual history navigation (up/down arrows)
- Custom implementation in interactive.rs
- Doesn't leverage bubbles::List features

## Benefits of bubbles::List
- Built-in keyboard navigation (up/down/home/end/pgup/pgdn)
- Filtering support (could enable history search)
- Consistent behavior with other charmed_rust components
- Less code to maintain

## Implementation
1. Create HistoryList wrapper around bubbles::List
2. Store input history as List items
3. Wire up to existing up/down key handling
4. Remove manual history_index tracking

## Test Plan
- Unit test: history navigation works as before
- Manual test: verify up/down/home/end work
- Verify no behavior changes for existing users

## Files to Modify
- src/interactive.rs (replace manual history with List)

## Risk
- Low - bubbles::List is battle-tested
- Maintain backwards compatibility with existing keybindings","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:09:12.322493675Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T21:49:53.503674421Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T21:49:53.503611003Z","close_reason":"Completed: replace manual input history navigation with bubbles::List-backed HistoryList; update /history output; add regression test; gates green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31hg","depends_on_id":"bd-20lm","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:09:12.330362116Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-31j","title":"Generate per-extension conformance report (machine + human)","description":"Background:
- We need a consumable summary of results for stakeholders and future self.

Steps:
- Produce a report that lists each extension, version, runtime tier, and pass/fail.
- Include failure reasons and links to fixtures/logs.
- Ensure report can be regenerated automatically.

Acceptance:
- Report is updated by running a single documented command.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
- [ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead
- [ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
- [ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
- [ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
- [ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes

Fix path enforcement in extension hostcall stack and re-run targeted test + gates.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-25T08:17:48.263044077Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T08:54:11.133361255Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T08:54:11.133334636Z","close_reason":"Completed: added __pi_host_check_write_access bridge guard and verified adversarial/fs/package-manager tests with rch","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-31ylu","title":"PARITY-V2: CLI Flag Parity Validation — exhaustive comparison of all flags between implementations","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:44:08.574492834Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-15T00:02:34.041846428Z","closed_at":"2026-02-15T00:02:34.041821912Z","close_reason":"Completed: added exhaustive CLI parity matrix report test and extension flag pass-through validation test; quality gates passed via rch","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","parity","testing","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31ylu","depends_on_id":"bd-2cd9b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:44:40.558786087Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":1557,"issue_id":"bd-31ylu","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## PARITY-V2: CLI Flag Parity Validation

### Purpose
Exhaustive test that every CLI flag in pi-mono works in our Rust port.

### Test Design
1. Extract ALL flags from pi-mono src/cli/args.ts
2. For each flag, verify it is accepted by our CLI (src/cli.rs)
3. For extension-registered flags (after CLI.1), verify they pass through
4. Generate a parity report: { flag, pi_mono_status, rust_status, notes }

### Full Flag List (from pi-mono)
- --provider, --model, --api-key, --system-prompt, --append-system-prompt
- --thinking, --continue/-c, --resume/-r, --mode, --print/-p
- --no-session, --session, --session-dir, --models/--list-models
- --no-tools, --tools, --extension/-e, --no-extensions
- --skill, --no-skills, --prompt-template, --no-prompt-templates
- --theme, --no-themes, --export, --verbose, --help/-h, --version/-v
- Extension-registered flags (dynamic, tested via test extension)

### Acceptance Criteria
- All pi-mono flags are accepted by Rust CLI
- A test extension can register a flag and user can pass it on CLI
- Parity report generated showing 100% coverage
- Test runs in CI","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:48:11Z"},{"id":1591,"issue_id":"bd-31ylu","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Differentiation from DROPIN-174

This bead (V2) is the QUICK SMOKE TEST run after CLI.1 implementation. It verifies:
- All pi-mono flags accepted by Rust CLI
- Extension-registered flags pass through correctly
- Parity report shows 100% coverage

DROPIN-174 (now depends on V2) is the COMPREHENSIVE test suite covering CLI + config + session + errors with structured logging and regression detection.

V2 = "do all flags work?" (enumeration + pass/fail). DROPIN-174 = "do all cross-surface behaviors match?" (deep behavior verification).","created_at":"2026-02-14T19:00:42Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-320","title":"Implement JS compatibility pipeline (bundle -> runtime)","description":"Background:
- Many extensions are JS/TS; we need a compatibility tier to run them as-is.
- Assume **QuickJS has no WebAssembly**: JS compatibility must be hybrid (QuickJS for JS + wasmtime-backed PiWasm for wasm).

Steps:
- Lock the JS execution strategy for the pinned sample:
  - QuickJS bytecode for JS execution.
  - PiWasm bridge (bd-1ry) for `globalThis.WebAssembly` expectations.
  - JS→WASM is optional/future; do not block sample success on it.
- Implement build pipeline integration + caching by hash (extc/SWC) and artifact metadata.
- Provide/ensure shims for legacy APIs (Node core + globals) map into connector hostcalls.
- Integrate with the QuickJS runtime and connector dispatcher.
- Ensure module loading contract supports shim modules and deterministic resolution.

Acceptance (hard):
- **All 16 extensions in `docs/extension-sample.json` run unmodified** (no manual source edits), end-to-end under the conformance/E2E harness.

Notes:
- Node core shims: bd-3d0 + bd-2sr.
- WebAssembly bridge: bd-1ry.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
- [ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead
- [ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
- [ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
- [ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
- [ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes

We have 60/60 official extensions running unmodified through the JS compatibility pipeline. The build pipeline (SWC TS→JS, QuickJS execution, virtual modules, bare aliases) is fully implemented. CJS-to-ESM conversion works. Closing.","created_at":"2026-02-05T21:17:11Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-321a","title":"Extension session connector integration (interactive + RPC)","description":"# Background
Extensions rely on session operations to:
- read the current session state (model/thinking/streaming flags, session id/name/file)
- enumerate messages/entries/branch
- mutate session metadata and content (set_name, append_message, append_entry, set_model, set_thinking_level, set_label)

In the Hostcall ABI these should be routed as `host_call` with `method = session` and a connector-specific `params` shape.

`src/extensions.rs` defines the `ExtensionSession` trait and `dispatch_hostcall_session()` already supports a rich set of ops + aliases.

# Gap (Concrete, Observed)
We currently have **two** `ExtensionSession` implementations with **different semantics**:
- Interactive: `InteractiveExtensionSession` (`src/interactive.rs`)
- Non-interactive / agent session handle: `SessionHandle` (`src/session.rs`)

Mismatch examples (these are the real parity risks):
- `get_state`:
  - interactive returns a rich object (e.g. `model`, `thinkingLevel`, `isStreaming`, `isCompacting`, `sessionId`, `sessionFile`, `sessionName`, `autoCompactionEnabled`, `messageCount`, ...)
  - `SessionHandle` returns only `{sessionFile, sessionName}`
- `get_messages`:
  - interactive filters to message entries on the current path + specific message types
  - `SessionHandle` returns all `SessionEntry::Message` across the entire session, not branch-scoped
- `get_entries` / `get_branch`:
  - interactive returns entries for the current path; branch is treated as current path
  - `SessionHandle` returns all entries for `get_entries`, but branch is only current path
- Persistence:
  - interactive saves immediately when `save_enabled`
  - `SessionHandle` mutates in-memory but does not save

Net: extensions can observe different state shapes + ordering depending on mode, which breaks compatibility and makes conformance/E2E flaky.

# Goal
Implement the session connector surface used by extensions and wire it end-to-end with **identical semantics** in:
- interactive TUI mode
- RPC mode (`pi --mode rpc`)

# Deliverables
1) **Contract (spec-first)**
- Define exact op names + params + return shapes (including allowed aliases).
- Pin field names + defaults to legacy expectations.

2) **Shared semantics**
- Unify the "extension-visible session state" builder so interactive + RPC produce the same shape.
- Enforce deterministic ordering for enumeration APIs.
- Define persistence rules (when session mutations must be saved) so extensions do not observe "worked but not persisted" behavior.

3) **Hostcall ABI wiring**
- Route `session.*` through the shared hostcall dispatcher (bd-1uy.1.1) so:
  - capability derivation + policy are consistent
  - timeouts/cancellation are enforced
  - error mapping uses taxonomy-only codes (`timeout|denied|io|invalid_request|internal`)

4) **Tests + E2E**
- Unit tests for each op (success + invalid_request + denied + timeout/cancel where applicable).
- Cross-mode parity tests (interactive vs RPC vs protocol adapter where applicable).
- E2E scenarios with JSONL logs + deterministic artifacts per bd-4u9.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Sample extensions that rely on session APIs run unmodified.
- Behavior is deterministic under harness control.
- Hostcall error codes are taxonomy-only.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T03:15:34.308214738Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T20:08:36.078957976Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T20:08:36.078917360Z","close_reason":"All 5 children closed: spec, implementations, taxonomy, conformance tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["extensions","hostcall","interactive","rpc","session"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-321a","depends_on_id":"bd-1uy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T03:15:34.315426860Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
